passing_score_scale: Scaled 0–400. Passing score varies by jurisdiction (most 260–270; NY requires 266).
exam_format: Multi-component exam administered over two days
exam_price_notes: Bar exam fees are set by each state bar board; typically $200–$1,000+. NCBE charges state boards per-candidate fees.

testing_centers.notes: MPRE: computer-based at Prometric test centers or online proctored. UBE/MBE: administered by individual state bar boards at designated test sites; increasingly computer-based via ExamSoft.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. ID must match bar exam or NCBE registration exactly.
exam_rules: Closed-book. No notes or reference materials. On-screen calculator provided for some calculations. UBE: essays and MPT tasks allow basic calculator. MPRE: no supplementary materials. Personal electronic devices not permitted.
renewal_options: Bar license renewal requirements set by individual state bar boards. Typically requires Continuing Legal Education (CLE) credits annually. Annual bar dues also required.

prerequisites.description: Juris Doctor (JD) degree from an ABA-accredited law school (required in most UBE jurisdictions). Some states have alternative pathways.
Character and fitness review by state bar board required

retake_policy.notes: UBE: retake policies set by individual state bar boards; most allow unlimited attempts. MPRE: offered 3 times per year (March, August, November); may be taken unlimited times.

domains.name: Multistate Bar Examination (MBE) — 50% of UBE score
Multistate Essay Examination (MEE) — 30% of UBE score
Multistate Performance Test (MPT) — 20% of UBE score

official_study_resources.title: NCBE Official Practice Exams
official_study_resources.notes: Official NCBE practice MBE questions and released MEE/MPT materials
